I stayed for 2 nights in Jan and checked out earlier than planned. I was assured the hotel will refund the extra nights accordingly. However, after 1 month the refund is still not completed with the right amount. On top of that, I have sent 4 emails inquiring about this and only the 1st was replied. I cannot imagine how difficult it is to solve such a routine problem.

If you are on vacation pick a better place to stay. If you just need lodging for a few days then this place is okay. Rooms were small but clean. Changed rooms because the first room smelled like mildew and both views in the rooms were underwhelming. Pool had murky water and limited seating. The nicest staff members were the valet guys. Went to the bar/restaurant near the front desk and was never given a menu or welcomed. Someone only came by to say they were closing in an hour and I'd have to leave. Overall okay for 3 days but wouldn't stay here again.

We have stayed at this hotel on 3 different occasions.  In general, we like the location.However, why put a SAFE in a room which cannot be locked or can be carried off?  None of our rooms had a bolted safe.  It seems a little strange.  The front desk staff didn’t offer any other options to store your belongings safely.If having a safe is important, you might want to stay somewhere else.  If it is not needed, the Hotel June offers clean accommodations with a friendly staff.

Room at 2 stars = thermostat broken, unable to cool room from 75 degrees.  Massive problem.  Room hot and incredibly noisy due to planes taking off all night long...like planes seem to be right outside the window.  Had I known this, I would not have stayed here.  Rooms are quite clean.Service = unsure who's working here at the front desk...no one answers the phone.  Could not find anyone to address heat in room.  And I think all the employees for the two Baja inspired restaurants might actually be in Baja.  Tried to order a cocktail and was told "sorry, bartender is on a 30 minute break".  Ummm, at 5:00 PM?  Woman drinking her margarita looked at me in disbelief as to what I was told.  Pretty unreal (terrible) service.Location = sucks.  See comment on planes and noise above.Would be a really nice hotel if....oh, never mind.I DO NOT recommend this hotel.
Toilet kept plugging.   They have a pool but they don’t   Take care of it.   No towels available.
Unbearable airplane noise. Booked for work and got absolutely zero sleep. Extremely loud. How can anyone stay here.NEVER AGAIN.

WARNING IF YOU STAY HERE YOU MAY BE USING USED TOILETRIES PLEASE READ.Have been to SEVERAL hotels around SoCal but none quite ask sneaky as this one. They put up a really aesthetic front however they are not welcoming whatsoever, claim to give you a “$50” meal credit that covers one breakfast (got a chia pudding, two coffees, and a breakfast sandwich, hardly a breakfast) . $55 per day for valet but even self parking* and all they do is park it at Ralph’s.  Worst of all. Before I checked out, I was interested in the Aesop toiletries so I asked the front desk attendant if I can purchase the ones in our room or if I would have to buy new ones. She said I’d get new ones however I was free to take the ones up in my room. I took the toiletries. Hours later I received a text from front desk telling me they will be charging me $318 for taking them. I explained my situation and said I would have left them if I knew but I was told otherwise they could even check footage and that I’d rather ship them back than be charged that amount and they said to go ahead and ship it back. I personally would be upset if I was using someone else’s shipped back soaps/lotions/toiletries at a hotel but maybe that’s just me. Just know if you do stay here after I post this review, you could very well be using my (or for that matter, anyone else’s) used soaps/lotions/toiletries if this is standard. Overall 1 star solely because the sandwich we had for breakfast that cost me my entire “hotel credit” was pretty good.

BUYER BEWARE! They charge $55 to valet park and that is the ONLY PARKING OPTION. They did NOT disclose this in ANY booking details fine-print on the Hotels.com website. They even tried to charge it twice but I caught it. The hotel was lovely and so was the room, but this felt like a bait-and-switch. I'd stay elsewhere. That cost is the highest I've seen EVER.  General staff was very nice. Lower ratings due to lack of disclosure and unwillingness to refund. Ignored my messages.

Beware. This hotel keeps your credit card information on file and commits fraud. I booked this hotel for Oct. 17-20. I canceled my trip to LA as well as this reservation. One month later, I looked at my credit card only to find that the hotel charged my card on Nov. 16! I emailed the hotel to explain, and the manager said it was for one night in October due to cancelation! I explained that No. 1, I have my receipt l, which says zero. He then sent me a non-existent email address and asked me to prove that I received a cancelation that said I owed nothing. I’m not sure what this hotel’s problem is. But I am flagging this for all people who care about what a hotel does with their personal information. Once you book this hotel, it indefinitely keeps your credit card information on file, and it may charge you weeks later.

On the upside, the room was fine--clean, comfortable. Some (not huge) negative points: 1) When checking in, it seems that valet parking is the only option (at $50/day). When asked if there were other options, we were told that there was also "street parking." While that is true, it is a bit misleading because immediately behind the hotel, there is a parking structure where you can park for free. 2) The online information on how to use the room's amenities did not include (that we could find) information about how to adjust the amount of opening of automatic window shades. 3) The small card on the bathroom counter that indicates that the hand soap and body lotion are available for purchase seems to imply that the two open containers on the counter can be taken by guests; when asked about this at the front desk, we were told that that is not the case. The card in the room could use some editing to make that clear. 4) There are cartons of spring water in the room, but there's no indication (that we could see) that these are complimentary, which they are. Again, it would be nice for guests to know that.
Breakfast comes in a cardboard box. Very very average.

I booked a one-night stay at this hotel and, unfortunately, my experience was disappointing. The hotel is located within a plaza that includes a Ralphs and several other businesses. However, the only parking option offered is valet at $48 per night. We were advised to either use valet or take our chances with street parking, which felt inconvenient and unexpected.Additionally, we were informed that because we booked through a third-party site and were charged a deposit fee, we would receive two complimentary drink vouchers. We never received the vouchers at check-in, and when we attempted to redeem the drinks, we were told there was no record of them on our reservation. Fortunately, the front desk staff member (a young woman with cute nails) working the night shift was kind enough to help resolve the issue.We were also told the bar would be open until 11:00 PM. When we went down around 8:30 PM, the bar was already closed, which was frustrating.Overall, the hotel itself has a nice aesthetic, and the room was clean with functioning heat and AC. However, the lack of clear communication, unexpected fees, and inconsistent service made the stay underwhelming. The room could also use better lighting. I expected a more polished and accommodating experience for the price and reviews.Big shoutout to the young, ginger man working the mid-day shift, the young woman working the night shift, and the two men working valet.

Our stay was initially straight forward and comfortable so I am disappointed I have to write this review:My friends and I came to celebrate my birthday for the weekend and originally booked a double queen room. The morning of our check in date I noticed a promo for the 2 bedroom studio which would only cost a bit more than the double queen.Once we arrived at the hotel, the person at the front desk said they would assist me with cancelling the reservation for the double queen while I made a new reservation for the 2 bedroom studio. I was assured the double queen was cancelled and was handed keys to the 2 bedroom studio. We didn't think twice about it and enjoyed our stay.It wasn't until the day we checked out that things went downhill-- I got charged for both rooms (double queen AND 2 bedroom studio) and they are refusing to refund me for the room we never stayed in! It has been like pulling teeth to get in touch with a hotel manager because they are 'in a meeting' or ' with another hotel guest.' I am going to end up needing to request a chargeback from my credit card company, which is such an unnecessary inconvenience.This has left a bad taste in my mouth and I do not know if I can recommend this hotel to others. It is very unfortunate because I have had such a high regard for any hotels associated with the Proper hotels until now.

Système de valet pas au point, très pénible quand il y a du monde. les valets sont les seuls personnels sympathiques. Ambiance générale très moyenne, énergie bizarre et hotel un peu prétentieux (on vous propose carrément une résurrection aromatique pour vous laver les mains...)

I stayed at the hotel for 1 night. They took a high amount of price and lied not to pay a deposit. Then they realized that I was right, they lied differently not to pay again. I definitely don't recommend it.

Super disappointed with this hotel. The toilet was extremely dirty when I arrived, and I really needed to use the bathroom. We called the front desk to have it cleaned or to change rooms, and they said they would send someone — but nobody came until we called a second time, about 20 minutes later. You can’t trust a hotel that doesn’t check the bathrooms in their rooms — that’s basic. I do not recommend this place. Save yourself the trouble.They also treated me very poorly. It was my first time staying at this hotel, recommended through my Capital One credit card. When I was checking out, I noticed they had chocolate chip cookies (my favorite!), so I asked how I could get some. The front desk clerk, very serious, told me they were only for VIP guests or people celebrating anniversaries or weddings.I mentioned that I was actually staying for my birthday — my birthday had been the day before, when I checked in — and she asked if I had indicated it in my reservation. But since I booked through Capital One, there’s no option to add notes or special requests. We told her that, and she said she would ask the manager. She made a call, and apparently, the manager said no. My husband even asked if we could buy the cookies, and she still refused to sell them. The whole situation easily took about five minutes.She turned what could have been a small, kind gesture into something unnecessarily unpleasant. It would have been so easy just to offer the cookies for my birthday or as a first-time guest. Instead, she made me feel like I wasn’t “VIP.”And it didn’t end there — as we were leaving after checkout, another front desk employee stopped us, saying he would “take care of the cookie issue.” But instead of just giving them to me, they spent another 3–5 minutes trying to find a code to charge my room $10 for them. It was so awkward. My husband just wanted to treat me to my favorite dessert.After such a ridiculous incident — on top of being welcomed with a dirty toilet and having to wait for it to be cleaned — I might have overlooked everything if they had simply given me the cookies and said “Happy Birthday.” But clearly, this hotel knows nothing about cleanliness or hospitality.I do NOT recommend this hotel. I suggest staying somewhere that actually values cleanliness and hospitality.

This is my home away from home when I visit LA. The staff here are the kindest, most helpful people. Corey, the manager, goes above and beyond to accommodate my requests. Jayson and all the front desk ladies (Keana, Amy, etc) over the years have been so nice and kind.The rooms are great. Beds are comfortable, lots of natural light with windows that open in each room (fresh air in a hotel room is a must for me as I have sensitivity to scents, cleaning product odors, shower dampness, etc). I love the esthetic in each room with the natural wood and Japanese/Scandinavian bed frame (perfect for mornings IYKYK). They have Aesop shower products (favorite) and water bottles in every room. The half shower glass wall does tend to make the floor wet which ends up wasting a lot of towels.Housekeeping does a great job though. Food is tasty - Caravan and Scenic route have good on site selections. The pool area is super cute with a chill club vibe and nice for lounging with chairs and cabanas.The gym has all the latest state of the art fitness equipment, including Tonal, Peloton bikes, Woodway treadmills, along with the normal equipment that you see. The air quality can be a bit of an issue at times: I’ve often noticed off gassing smell from the chemicals in the rubber or pvc equipment. The windows do open but they’re on the second floor so if there’s a lot of traffic on Lincoln in front which is often the case, it is not great to exercise in. Perhaps air filters would help air quality. I end up going during non-peak traffic hours so i can open the windows. I like to use the foam rollers after a long cross country flight and stretch.They do have bikes for the beach and are next to Ralph’s grocery store so it’s super convenient. All around great hotel - they’ve thought of everything.One thing i wish they had were more lamps in the rooms. At night the only sources of light are the bedside lamps or bathroom lights. It’s hard to unpack or see my luggage in the dark unless i drag it into the bathroom or find a room that has an extra wall lamp. I typically arrive 9pm coming from the east coast and more light would make a huge difference.

I was in LA for business and needed to be close to LAX. One of my clients recommended Hotel June, so my coworker and I decided to book rooms. Upon arriving after a long travel day, I was immediately impressed by the staff for their hospitality.I loved the "boutique" feel of the hotel and the rooms were clean + had all of the amenities needed for my short stay. For me to take time to write a review for a hotel these days is saying a lot.  I'll start by sharing I am not easily impressed (especially when it comes to lodging in the United States these days) and I do not hand out compliments easily.  That said, the Hotel June West LA is hands down one of the best kept secrets for a stay near LAX with easy access to Santa Monica and UCLA/Westwood/BH.  While very close to and super convenient to LAX, Hotel June is not your typical 'airport' hotel.  It is separated from the pack and provides a consistent clean, comfortable and welcoming stay for a great value.  Great location situated in Playa Vista (take a morning walk to the nearby Starbucks) or a longer walk to the Pacific Ocean.  Coffee shops, eateries and a grocery market within walking distance.  Rooms are simple and comfortable and the shower water pressure is amazing (unheard of in California!) and high end Aesop bath products.  No noisy window air conditioner units at Hotel June and a window that opens if you appreciate fresh air.  Super comfy mattress, great pillows and cozy linens and towels.  Hotel June is now my new go to property when visiting LA.  I am happy to spend a little more time in my car driving to where a need to go in order to be comfortable and sleep well.  This past stay I had the opportunity to dine for dinner at Caravan Swim Club and the fish tacos were amazing!  Don't miss them.In closing, I'd like to give a shout out to a few staff I have encountered during my recent stays.  Cory (front of house manager, I believe) is a sharp and professional leader and it clearly shows in his staff.  Kevin who checked me in was a delight and provides guests great first impression.  Two front desk overnight staff during my stay (sorry I do not recall their names) provided excellent service related to a noise issue in the middle of the night.
